task_completion.description: The target image is opened in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP application is open with a photograph (likely a landscape or nature scene from Unsplash) visible in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Sharpen or Unsharp Mask filter dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Sharpen' or 'Unsharp Mask' is visible on the screen, typically accessed via Filters -> Enhance.

task_completion.description: The sharpen filter is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas appears noticeably sharper with enhanced edge definition, or the filter dialog is shown being confirmed (e.g., clicking 'OK').

task_completion.description: The modified image is saved or exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image', 'Save', or 'Save As' dialog is visible and being confirmed, or a notification/status bar indicates the file has been saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical interface to process the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within the GIMP application window rather than using terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent used a legitimate sharpen filter.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Sharpen' or 'Unsharp Mask' tool is explicitly used, rather than manually painting over edges or just adjusting global contrast.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open running Python scripts or shell commands to process the image automatically.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The environment runs GIMP 2.10 on a GNOME desktop. The agent must use a sharpen filter (typically Filters -> Enhance -> Sharpen (Unsharp Mask)). The final image must have >=10% sharpness improvement and >=5% edge strength improvement, without excessive noise (noise ratio < 3.0). The agent should save/export the image after applying the filter.
